recap: Stephen Eustaquio broke South African hearts with a stunning stoppage-time volley, sending Canada through to the Round of 16 with a 1-0 win at SoFi Stadium — their first knockout-stage victory in World Cup history. A fiercely contested match looked destined for extra time until Eustaquio, who plays his club football nearby for LAFC, rifled a volley from outside the box into the bottom corner in the 92nd minute. Goalkeeper Ronwen Williams had been outstanding with five saves to keep Bafana Bafana in it, but Canada held firm in the frantic final moments to advance.
